text:
{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"2","subject":"Design new skills: concrete skill proposals for the cat clicker","description":"Based on the research findings, design 15-25 NEW skill nodes for the cat clicker game's skill tree. The game currently has 23 nodes across 3 branches (Pulse, Cat Value/Size, Session/Combo) with 5 keystones.

CURRENT GAME CONTEXT:
- 15-second sessions where you click cats with a circular pulse ring
- Stats: pulse_radius, pulse_interval, cat_value, cat_size, spawn_interval, session_duration, combo_decay, combo_multiplier, critical_chance
- Existing keystones: Chain Harvest, Critical Pulse, Cat Frenzy, Magnet (stub), Pulse Echo (stub)
- Architecture: TreeNodeData resources with effect handlers (strategy pattern)
- Effects are either Flat (additive) or Perc (multiplicative), or Unlock (boolean mechanic toggle)

DESIGN REQUIREMENTS:
- Each skill must have: name, effect description, stat it modifies (or new stat), number of levels, approximate cost range, which branch it belongs to, parent node
- Skills must create MEANINGFUL CHOICES - no obvious best path
- Include at least 3-4 new KEYSTONE mechanics that fundamentally change gameplay
- Design synergies BETWEEN branches to reward hybrid builds
- Create "build identity" - a player investing in one path should FEEL different from another
- Consider the 15-second session constraint - skills need to be impactful in short bursts
- Include at least 2 "risk/reward" nodes that trade one stat for another
- Design for dopamine: power spikes, satisfying breakpoints, "just one more node" hooks
- New stat categories to consider: luck, multi-hit, area damage, cat attraction, earnings multiplier, golden cat chance, session rewards

Produce a detailed design document with the full skill tree layout (existing + new nodes), organized by branch with clear parent-child relationships.","assignedBy":"designer","timestamp":"2026-04-03T12:39:15.590Z"}
Judge here — when you're ready, please write your skill design deliverable to C:\Users\Patryk\Projects\idle-1\.planning\skill-research\SKILL_DESIGN.md. I'm ready to review as soon as it lands.
